info.name: 10. đź”Ś Real-Time Sockets
info.description: API collection detailing the real-time Socket.IO chat engine events, emits, and broadcasts.

request.description:
## Socket.IO Real-Time Chat Engine

This system allows for low-latency real-time chat interactions. Sockets automatically join an invisible personal channel `user:{user_id}` on connection so they can act as global listeners for any incoming message, regardless of whether a particular chat window is open.

### đź”Ś Connection
| Specification | Details |
|---------------|---------|
| Server URL | `http://localhost:3000` (or `https://` in prod) |
| Authentication | Must pass the JWT during initialization using `auth: { token: "Bearer ..." }` |

---

## â¬†ď¸Ź Client EMITS (Action Triggers)

These are events emitted *by the frontend* to trigger backend actions. Each emit requires a payload and supports a callback (Acknowledgment) containing exactly `{ success, data, error }`.

### 1. `conversation:join`
Joins the specific room for a conversation. Call this when the user clicks/navigates into a specific chat view.
**Payload:** 
```json
{
  "conversation_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109ca"
}
```
**Ack Response (Success):** 
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": { "conversation_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109ca" }
}
```

### 2. `message:new`
Sends a new message to the chat.
**Payload:** 
```json
{
  "conversation_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109ca",
  "text": "Hey! Check this mix.",
  "shared_entity": {
      "type": "Track",
      "id": "64f1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b9c0d1"
  }
}
```
**Ack Response (Success):** 
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "conversation_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109ca",
    "last_message_at": "2026-04-14T15:45:00.000Z",
    "message": {
        "_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109ee",
        "conversation_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109ca",
        "sender_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109uu",
        "text": "Hey! Check this mix.",
        "shared_entity": { "type": "Track", "id": "64f1a2b3c4d5e6f7a8b9c0d1" },
        "is_read": false,
        "read_at": null,
        "createdAt": "2026-04-14T15:45:00.000Z",
        "updatedAt": "2026-04-14T15:45:00.000Z",
        "__v": 0
    }
  }
}
```

### 3. `conversation:read`
Marks all unread messages in the active chat view as read.
**Payload:** 
```json
{
  "conversation_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109ca"
}
```
**Ack Response (Success):** 
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "conversation_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109ca",
    "reader_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109uu",
    "marked_count": 2,
    "read_at": "2026-04-14T15:46:00.000Z"
  }
}
```

---

## â¬‡ď¸Ź Server BROADCASTS (Listeners)

These are events broadcasted *by the backend* to the frontend. The frontend must set up `.on()` listeners to process them.

### 1. `message:new`
Triggered when someone sends a message. The frontend will receive this broadcast in **two places:**
- If they are actively viewing the chat (`conversation:${id}` room)
- Anywhere else in the app (via their personal `user:${id}` room notification banner)
*Note: Your frontend should deduplicate events using `message._id` to prevent double-rendering.*

**Payload Broadcast:**
```json
{
  "conversation_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109ca",
  "last_message_at": "2026-04-14T15:45:00.000Z",
  "message": {
      "_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109ee",
      "conversation_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109ca",
      "sender_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109uu",
      "text": "Hey! Check this mix.",
      "is_read": false,
      "createdAt": "2026-04-14T15:45:00.000Z"
  }
}
```

### 2. `conversation:read`
Triggered when the person you are talking to opens the chat or explicitly marks it as read.
**Payload Broadcast:**
```json
{
  "conversation_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109ca",
  "reader_id": "60d0fe4f5311236168a109cb",
  "marked_count": 2,
  "read_at": "2026-04-14T15:46:00.000Z"
}
```
